description:
You are working as a container-platform specialist and have been asked to prove that a
very small “micro-service” can be brought online inside the current Linux container.

Your job:

1. Create a directory EXACTLY at  
   /home/user/microservices/webroot

2. Inside that directory create a file called  
   index.html  
   whose entire *exact* contents are the single line (without the quotes)  
   Hello, container world!

   • There must be **no leading or trailing spaces**.  
   • A single trailing newline (0x0A) at the end of the file is acceptable.

3. From inside /home/user/microservices/webroot start a plain Python HTTP
   server on port 8080 that serves the current directory.  
   • The server must keep running in the background after your command returns
     so that other tooling can still reach it.  
   • Use any standard technique (e.g. python3 -m http.server 8080 &) but do NOT
     change the port.

4. In the same shell session verify the service by issuing one HTTP GET request
   to http://localhost:8080/ and redirecting the **body** of the reply to the file

   /home/user/output/webserver_response.log

   The log file’s content must be **exactly**:

   Hello, container world!

   A single trailing newline (0x0A) is acceptable but no other text or spacing
   is allowed.

What the automated tests will check:

• /home/user/microservices/webroot/index.html exists and contains the precise
  text above.  
• /home/user/output/webserver_response.log exists and contains the precise text
  above.  
• The HTTP server is (optionally) still listening on TCP port 8080, but the
  presence and correctness of webserver_response.log are the primary pass
  criteria.

Use any method you like, but you should not need more than 2-3 shell commands
in total to accomplish the task.
truth:
Expected file system state after successful completion:

1. File: /home/user/microservices/webroot/index.html  
   Content (shown with ⏎ for the optional single trailing LF):  
   "Hello, container world!⏎"  
   Size: 22 bytes if the newline is present, 21 bytes if not.

2. File: /home/user/output/webserver_response.log  
   Content must be byte-for-byte identical to index.html (same optional trailing
   newline rule).

3. The process table should include a process whose command line contains:
   "python3 -m http.server 8080"  
   (or the equivalent CPython executable path) and it should be owned by the
   current user.

The automated verification script will:

A. cat /home/user/microservices/webroot/index.html and compare against the exact
   string "Hello, container world!\n?"  
B. cat /home/user/output/webserver_response.log and compare against the same
   exact string.  
C. Optionally confirm that a TCP listener exists on 0.0.0.0:8080 or 127.0.0.1:8080.

No other files or directories are required for the test to pass.
